hypothesis: Integrating a persistent semantic and episodic memory module into the experiment setup and research teams will enable robust zero-shot generalization to unseen zeolite structures and adsorbates, such that, on 10 novel structure–adsorbate tasks, the memory-augmented system achieves at least 95% correctness in generating RASPA input files and at least 90% reproducibility of HOA and adsorption isotherm predictions, versus ≤80% correctness and ≤75% reproducibility in the memoryless baseline.
